source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260520 우크라이나(Licarno) 23G, P1 - 이창민 팀장님/260527 1차 제출본/3.2 [Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector] Technical Document/A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports/1. [23G series] IEC 60601-1 CB Certi & Test report (ed. 3.2).pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report REP028549 with IEC 60601-1-6 Test Report REP028549_1 and Attachments
document.documentType: CB Scheme safety and usability test report with national deviations and user manual attachment
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. REP028549; IEC 60601-1-6 Report No. REP028549_1; User manual A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.1; software validation report revision 0

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk documents passed IEC 62304 software lifecycle review, IEC 60601-1 national deviations, IEC 60601-1-6 usability compliance, and user manual safety/specification information for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory submissions and CB Scheme evidence for basic safety, essential performance, software lifecycle, usability engineering, national deviations, labeling, cybersecurity, and product specification claims.
keyPoints: Software was classified as IEC 62304 safety class B and evaluated against software development, verification, release, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution clauses.
No legacy software was considered in the IEC 62304 attachment.
IEC 60601-1 national deviations were addressed for the United States, Canada, Japan, and Republic of Korea.
IEC 60601-1-6 report REP028549_1 was issued by Nemko Korea on 2024-02-20 and states the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6 and EN 60601-1-6 requirements.
The usability engineering file was checked against IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020 with referenced usability validation and engineering reports.
The user manual identifies intended use, contraindications, cybersecurity controls, IP56 ingress protection, electrical ratings, and detector technical specifications.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1-6 report lists applicant as H&abyz Co., Ltd. and manufacturer as same as applicant.

entities.name: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Report REP028549_1 lists Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. as the testing laboratory preparing the report and CB Testing Laboratory.

entities.name: A1717MCW
entities.evidence: Model/type reference in Report REP028549_1: A1717MCW; also pictured and described in the user manual.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: Model/type reference in Report REP028549_1: A1417MCW; also pictured and described in the user manual.

entities.name: F1417MCW
entities.evidence: Model/type reference in Report REP028549_1: F1417MCW; also pictured and described in the user manual.

entities.name: DEPS9601A
entities.evidence: Attachment pictures identify power supply box model DEPS9601A; ratings list DEPS9601A as 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Class I or 3 A, 24 V.

entities.name: BM090S15F
entities.evidence: Attachment pictures identify AC/DC adapter model BM090S15F.

concepts.name: IEC 62304 software safety class B
concepts.evidence: Software safety classification section states the software is classified as software class B and class B criterion is passed.

concepts.name: No legacy software considered
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 clause 4.4 Legacy Software states: No legacy software considered.

concepts.name: Software validation report A-TCF-5-131
concepts.evidence: IEC 62304 compliance items repeatedly reference Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), including chapters 1, 4, 8.3, 9, 11, and 12.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1 national deviations
concepts.evidence: Attachment 2 lists national deviations for United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1-6 usability report
concepts.evidence: Attachment 3 is an IEC 60601-1-6 test report, report number REP028549_1, for Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6 collateral standard usability.

concepts.name: IEC 62366-1 usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: Report states inspection of the usability engineering file verified compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020 and Annex I contains the usability engineering process checklist.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity wireless access controls
concepts.evidence: Manual says transmitted data is protected using WPA/WPAPSK/WPA2/WPA2PSK and random access is restricted by ID/PW for wireless network connection.

concepts.name: IP56 ingress protection
concepts.evidence: Manual states A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are rated IP56 water-resistant and dustproof according to IEC 60529, and device specification lists IP Range: IP56.

evidence.claim: The medical device software is classified as IEC 62304 software safety class B.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.3 states the software system is classified as software class B; class A and class C lines are N/A and the class B criterion has verdict P.

evidence.claim: The software safety class is documented in the risk management file and supporting software validation report.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.3(c) states the manufacturer documents the software safety class in the risk management file; evidence references Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), chapter 1, with verdict P.

evidence.claim: No legacy software was considered for this software compliance assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.4 Legacy Software remarks state: No legacy software considered, with N/A verdicts for legacy software activities.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer established a software development plan appropriate to the device software lifecycle.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.1.1 references Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), chapter 8.3, and passes requirements for development planning, lifecycle model, deliverables, traceability, and problem resolution.

evidence.claim: Software requirements included functional, interface, alarm/message, security, IT-network, maintenance, and regulatory requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2.2 lists software requirements including functional requirements, inputs/outputs, interfaces, software-driven alarms, SECURITY requirements, IT-network aspects, user maintenance, and regulatory requirements, all with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software requirements were verified for risk-control implementation, consistency, ambiguity avoidance, testability, unique identification, and traceability.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2.6 states software requirements implement system requirements including risk controls, do not contradict one another, avoid ambiguity, permit test criteria, are uniquely identified, and traceable; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The software architecture was documented and verified against system/software requirements and interfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.3.1, 5.3.2, and 5.3.6 reference Software validation report chapters 6 and 7 and pass architecture, interface, and risk-control implementation verification requirements.

evidence.claim: Software integration, integration testing, regression testing, anomaly handling, and repeatable test records were assessed as passing.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.6.1 through 5.6.8 reference Software validation report chapter 9 and pass integration plan, integration records, integration testing, regression testing, test results, repeatability records, tester identification, and anomaly entry into problem resolution.

evidence.claim: Software system testing covered software requirements and included pass/fail criteria, traceability, software version, configurations, tools, date, and tester identity.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.7.1 through 5.7.5 reference Software validation report chapter 9 and pass requirements for test coverage, adequacy evaluation, anomaly process, traceability, test criteria, software version, hardware/software configuration, test tools, date, and tester identity.

evidence.claim: Software release controls included completed verification, residual anomalies, released software version, release environment, archived software/configuration items, and delivery protection against corruption or unauthorized change.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.8.1 through 5.8.8 reference Software validation report chapter 8.3 and pass release, anomaly, version, environment, completion, archive, and reliable delivery requirements.

evidence.claim: Software risk management was linked to ISO 14971 and included software hazard analysis, causes, risk controls, verification, and traceability.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 7.1 through 7.3 reference Software validation report chapter 11 and pass requirements for identifying software items contributing to hazardous situations in ISO 14971 risk analysis, documenting causes, defining risk controls, verifying risk controls, and traceability.

evidence.claim: Software change control and configuration management records were assessed as passing.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 8.1.3, 8.2.1 to 8.2.4, and 8.3 reference Software validation report chapters 8.3 and 12 and pass configuration-item documentation, approved change requests, change verification, traceability, and retrievable history records.

evidence.claim: Software problem resolution procedures and records were assessed as passing.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 9.1 through 9.8 reference Software validation report chapter 8.3 and pass problem report, criticality, investigation, safety evaluation, change request, relevant-party notification, resolution records, trend analysis, verification, retesting, and regression test documentation requirements.

evidence.claim: The IEC 62304 evidence mapping identifies software validation report A-TCF-5-131 revision 0 dated 2023-11-07 as the main manufacturer document.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software mapping lists multiple deliverables with title Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), revision 0, date 2023-11-07.

evidence.claim: Attachment pictures identify external, internal, PCB, power supply, adapter, battery, switch box, and exposure switch views for the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 1 Pictures of equipment includes external views for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW; internal views; PCB views; power supply box DEPS9601A; AC/DC adapter BM090S15F; Li-ion battery; switch box; and exposure switch.
evidence.pageTarget: Attachment 1 Pictures of equipment Pages 1-19 of 19

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 national deviations were included for United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 National deviations lists United States (US), Canada (CA), Japan (JP), and Korea (KR).

evidence.claim: For US national differences, the power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated 100-240 V~.
evidence.evidenceText: US national differences clause 4.10.2 notes the power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated as 100-240 V~ with verdict P.

evidence.claim: For US national differences, the equipment was marked as not an X-ray system for certain X-ray system-specific clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: US national differences clauses 6.6, 7.2.11, and 8.6.1 state Not an X-Ray system with N/A verdicts.

evidence.claim: For Canada national differences, Canadian 60601 collateral standards were considered, with no particular standards applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: Canada national differences clauses 1.3 and 1.4 state applicable Canadian 60601 collateral standards are considered and no particular standards applicable.

evidence.claim: For Japan national differences, JIS T 2304:2017 and JIS T 14971:2020 are listed as Japanese references corresponding to IEC 62304 and ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Japan national differences normative references list JIS T 2304:2017 Medical device software - Software life cycle processes and JIS T 14971:2020 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices.

evidence.claim: For Korea national differences, the power supply box and AC/DC adapter meet voltage and frequency requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Republic of Korea national differences state national supply voltages are 110, 220 V and 380 V; the power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated 100-240 V~ with verdict P; frequency range includes 60 Hz with verdict P.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-6 report REP028549_1 was issued by Nemko Korea on 2024-02-20 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Report REP028549_1 lists date of issue 2024-02-20, testing laboratory Nemko Korea Co., Ltd., test item description Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, and model/type reference A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 report used CB Scheme procedure and states the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6 and EN 60601-1-6 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing states test procedure CB Scheme and product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 + A2:2021.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 report states it is a process standard only with no testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing lists Tests performed: Process standard only, no testing.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering file demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 clause 4.2 states inspection of the usability engineering file verified that the manufacturer demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.

evidence.claim: The user manual contained the required use specification summary for IEC 60601-1-6 accompanying documents.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 clause 5 states the instructions for use shall contain a summary of the use specification and notes it is included in the user manual with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Usability engineering documentation included usability validation report A-TCF-5-107 and usability engineering report A-TCF-5-108.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62366-1 checklist references Usability validation report (A-TCF-5-107) and Usability engineering report (A-TCF-5-108) for process, risk-control, and file requirements with verdict P.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process included use specification, user-interface safety risk analysis, hazard-related use scenarios, user-interface specification, evaluation plan, implementation, and summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex I clauses 5.1 through 5.9 pass use specification, safety-related interface characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, hazard-related use scenarios, summative evaluation selection, user-interface specification, evaluation plan, implementation, and summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance was not considered.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 62366-1 checklist clause 5.10 states User Interface of Unknown Provenance was not considered, with N/A verdict.

evidence.claim: The user manual is A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.1 dated 15/09/2023 and is provided in English/Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 4 User manual title page states User manual for A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW, A-TCF-5-130 Version: 0.1 15/09/2023, Language: ENGLISH / KOREAN.

evidence.claim: The product is intended as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: User manual Intended purpose states A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is designed for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children and intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.

evidence.claim: The user manual contraindicates use for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Contraindications state not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application, and not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The detector applied part is the front panel and patient contact duration is less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Manual states the degree of protection is Type B applied part for the front panel and applied part/duration: patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The detectors include cybersecurity controls for wireless data and access.
evidence.evidenceText: Security section states A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW protects transmitted data using WPA/WPAPSK/WPA2/WPA2PSK variants and restricts random access by setting ID/PW for wireless network connection.

evidence.claim: The detectors are IP56 water-resistant and dustproof according to IEC 60529 when battery cover and wireless mode conditions are met.
evidence.evidenceText: Caution section states the models are rated IP56 according to IEC 60529, and IP56 is guaranteed when the battery cover is closed tightly while wired cable is disconnected and wireless mode is connected.

evidence.claim: The detector system is Class I or internally powered and has maximum 48 W power consumption.
evidence.evidenceText: Device specification lists rated voltage 100-240 V~, rated frequency 50/60 Hz, electric power consumption MAX 48W, and protection type Class I or internally powered.

evidence.claim: The detector supports wired and wireless digital flat panel operation with auto exposure detection.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Features state the models are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors and support auto exposure detection technology allowing use without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The detector sensor technology is indirect a-Si TFT with CsI scintillator, 16-bit A/D conversion, 140 um pixel pitch, and maximum limiting resolution 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical Features list sensor type a-Si TFT, scintillator CsI, pixel pitch 140um, limiting resolution max 3.57lp/mm, and A/D conversion 16 bits.

evidence.claim: Detector wireless communication supports IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac at 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical Features list Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac, 2.4GHz/5GHz.

evidence.claim: Detector battery performance is specified as 3 hours with a fully charged battery for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Battery section lists battery performance: A1717MCW 3 hours, A1417MCW 3 hours, F1417MCW 3 hours with full charged battery.

evidence.claim: The product is portable and received test items on 2023-08-25, with testing performed 2023-09-04 to 2023-10-06 for the IEC 60601-1-6 report.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item particulars list classification of installation and use as Portable; date of receipt 2023-08-25; dates of performance of tests 2023-09-04 to 2023-10-06.

reviewFlags.reason: This chunk contains later pages and attachments only; earlier certificate cover pages, full IEC 60601-1 report context, and any CB certificate details outside this chunk are not extracted.

Several source statements say the detector is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector while some national-deviation rows state 'Not an X-Ray system' for X-ray system-specific clauses; preserve this distinction when using the evidence.
IEC 60601-1-6 report states 'process standard only, no testing'; usability compliance is based on process/file inspection rather than physical testing.
